slides.title: Staying well-hydrated is linked to *slower aging*.
slides.sub: A simple daily habit may quietly protect your long-term health. Here is what the research suggests.

slides.title: Water and your biology
slides.body: In a large study, well-hydrated adults *aged more slowly* and had fewer chronic diseases than those who ran dry.

slides.title: Most run a little dry
slides.body: Many people are *mildly dehydrated* without noticing. Thirst lags, and busy days make it easy to forget.

slides.title: It touches everything
slides.body: Good hydration can support energy, skin and the load on *your heart and kidneys* over the years.

slides.title: Sip through the day
slides.body: Sip steadily instead of gulping all at once. Pale-yellow urine is a *simple, honest gauge*.

slides.title: Food and tea count
slides.body: Water-rich foods and *herbal tea also count*. Fruit, soup and vegetables quietly add to your total.

slides.title: Track the habits that *add years*.
slides.body: Vibe Health turns your water, food, sleep and movement into one simple daily score, so steady habits are easy to see.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
One of the simplest longevity habits is also the easiest to forget: staying well-hydrated. 💧

In research, well-hydrated adults aged more slowly. Sip steadily through the day, and let pale-yellow urine be your gauge. Food and herbal tea count too. 🔖

Vibe Health turns the small daily habits into one calm score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How Staying Hydrated Is Linked to Slower Aging
